title: The Memory Debt
idea: In a world where magic spells are fueled by personal memories, the most powerful sorcerers in history are also the most hollow, having sacrificed their entire childhoods and identities to achieve greatness. The protagonist is a young prodigy who realizes that to cast a 'life-saving' spell, they must forget the face of the person they are trying to save, meaning they will be the only one who doesn't remember their loved one's existence after the miracle is performed.
hook: They say the most powerful mages are 'empty' for a reason.
targetAudience: Gen Z and Millennial fantasy fans who enjoy dark, emotional storytelling and character-driven stakes.
viralAngle: It creates a moral dilemma that sparks heated debates in the comments: Is it worth achieving greatness if you lose the very reason you started? It resonates with the 'sacrifice' trope but subverts it by making the sacrifice the loss of the bond itself, which hits hard emotionally.
